src/parser

A tiny parser leaf used by the fixture repository to expose one concrete parsing path.

What this is

This directory contains a single Rust function, parse_answer, that takes &str input and returns a u32 by parsing the input directly and expecting the fixture input to be numeric.

Role in the whole

In a repository described as a fixture for glance-core tests, this leaf gives the test suite a minimal parsing surface to inspect: one exported function, one numeric conversion, and one fixed success assumption.

That makes the directory useful as a small, stable example of how a parser module can turn raw text into a typed answer.

Composition

This is a leaf directory with no child directories.

Its visible composition is a single module file defining one function, so the directory stays intentionally small and easy to reason about from the outside.

Seams and contracts

The load-bearing contract is the expectation that the input is numeric; the function does not recover from parse failure and instead uses expect with the message fixture input is numeric.

The seam is therefore narrow: callers hand in text, and the only supported outcome here is successful conversion into u32.

Where it can hurt you

The sharp edge is explicit failure on non-numeric input, because expect turns invalid text into a panic rather than a recoverable result.

That edge is acceptable in a fixture, but it means this code should only be exercised with inputs that satisfy the numeric contract baked into the function.
